title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
slides: SpaceX built an AI device prototype. What for?
It runs neural nets locally without cloud lag.
But its form factor looks suspiciously like a smartphone.
Your next phone could be a Starlink terminal with an AI brain.
Watch for SpaceX to skip carriers—not just phones.
thread_text:
SpaceX built a prototype.

It's an AI device that runs neural nets locally, no cloud lag.

It looks like a smartphone.

But its real job is to connect directly to Starlink satellites.

Your next phone could be a Starlink terminal.

With an AI brain baked in, no carrier needed.

Watch for SpaceX to skip carriers entirely.

Not just phones — any device could get this.

Ask yourself: when was the last time you needed a cell tower?

If this ships, your pocket changes the network.
video_script: It looks just like a phone.
Your next phone connects to Starlink.

script: It looks just like a phone.
Your next phone connects to Starlink.

instagram.caption:
SpaceX built a prototype AI device that runs neural nets locally, no cloud lag—and it looks suspiciously like a smartphone. 📱

It connects directly to Starlink satellites, skipping cell towers entirely. Your next phone could be a Starlink terminal with an AI brain baked in.

For anyone creating or publishing content, this means anytime, anywhere connectivity with local AI processing—no carrier, no dead zones.

When was the last time you actually needed a tower to post? 🛰️

For anyone creating or publishing content, this means anytime, anywhere connectivity with local AI processing—no carrier, no dead zones.

When was the last time you actually needed a tower to post? 🛰️

carousel_slides.description: SpaceX built an AI device running neural nets locally, no cloud lag. It connects directly to Starlink satellites.
The prototype looks like a smartphone but its real job is to connect to Starlink satellites as a terminal.
Starlink terminal with an AI brain means direct satellite connection, skipping traditional carriers entirely.
Any device could get this AI-powered Starlink link, not just phones. Carriers may become obsolete.
Check the link in bio to see how SpaceX is reshaping connectivity and why carriers should worry.
